name: OpenAdapt
tagline: Record desktop GUI demonstrations, train models, and automate workflows from one CLI
seoTitle: OpenAdapt - GUI Automation with Multimodal Models
metaDescription: OpenAdapt is an open-source adapter between large multimodal models and desktop/web GUIs. Record demos, train models, and evaluate agents from one CLI.
overview: OpenAdapt is an open-source software adapter that connects large multimodal models (LMMs) to traditional desktop and web GUIs. You record a demonstration of a task by performing it yourself, then OpenAdapt uses that recording to train models and run agents that can replay and automate the same GUI workflow.

It is aimed at developers and teams who want to automate repetitive desktop processes without scripting every click and keystroke by hand. Instead of writing brittle UI selectors, you show the tool what to do once and work from the captured demonstration.

As an agent framework for computer and browser use, OpenAdapt v1.0+ uses a modular meta-package design: the main openadapt package gives you a unified CLI, and focused sub-packages (capture, ml, evals, privacy, and more) are installed only when you need them.
features: Records GUI demonstrations (screen plus input events) that become training data for automation
Unified openadapt CLI covering capture, training, evaluation, and a dashboard server
Modular meta-package: install only the extras you need, such as [capture], [ml], [evals], or [privacy]
Trains models on your captures and evaluates them against benchmarks, or evaluates API agents
Optional PII/PHI scrubbing via the privacy package for sensitive recordings
Built-in viewer to open and inspect recorded captures

gettingStarted.intro: OpenAdapt requires Python 3.10+. Install the CLI with the extras you need, then record, train, and evaluate a task.

steps.heading: Install
steps.body: Install the minimal CLI, or pick an extra such as [capture] for recording, [ml] for training, or [all] for everything.

steps.heading: Record a demonstration
steps.body: Start a capture, perform the actions in your GUI, then press Ctrl+C to stop.

steps.heading: Train a model
steps.body: Train a model on the capture you just recorded.

steps.heading: Evaluate or view
steps.body: Run an evaluation against a benchmark, or open the viewer to inspect a recording.
steps.code:
openadapt eval run --checkpoint training_output/model.pt --benchmark waa
openadapt capture view my-task

useCases: Automate a repetitive desktop workflow by demonstrating it once instead of scripting every UI action
Build training data for GUI agents by recording screen and input demonstrations
Train and benchmark a computer-use model against tasks, then compare it with API agents
Capture sensitive desktop workflows with optional PII/PHI scrubbing before sharing recordings
